questions.question: What is the name of the algorithm used to search for an element in a sorted array?
questions.explanation: Binary Search is an efficient algorithm for finding an item from a sorted list of items. It works by repeatedly dividing in half the portion of the list that could contain the item, until you've narrowed down the possible locations to just one.

questions.question: Which of the following data structures is most suitable for storing a collection of objects with efficient retrieval?
questions.explanation: Hash Tables are data structures that store key-value pairs and provide efficient retrieval of objects using a unique key.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of searching for an element in a hash table, assuming the hash function is good?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of searching for an element in a hash table, assuming the hash function is good, is O(1).

questions.question: Which of the following operations can be performed on a tree data structure?
questions.explanation: Trees are data structures that support a variety of operations, including insert, delete, and search.

questions.question: What is the name of the data structure that consists of a collection of nodes, where each node contains a value and a reference to the next node?
questions.explanation: A linked list is a data structure where each element is a separate object, known as a node. Each node contains a value and a reference to the next node in the sequence.
